73.reason: [Error: When a duplicate word is found, the code copies it to d[q] and increments b1[q], but then q is incremented, leaving d[q] as a duplicate entry. This causes the same word to appear multiple times in the output with incorrect counts. The correct logic should only increment the count of the existing word without adding a new entry.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logic error: When a duplicate word is found, it copies the word to the next slot d[q] and increments b1[q]. More importantly, this approach creates a new entry for every duplicate instead of just incrementing the count of the existing unique word. The standard approach is to just increment b1[q-1] and NOT create a new entry in d.

50.reason: The logic for extracting words is flawed. It only processes a word when a non-letter character is encountered. If a line ends with a letter (which is common), the last word of that line is never extracted because the loop terminates without hitting the 'else' condition to trigger the copy. Additionally, it does not null-terminate the strings in c[s], leading to garbage data during comparison and output.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  The logic for extracting words is flawed. It only adds a word when a non-letter character is encountered. If a line ends with a letter (word), the last word is never added to array c because the loop terminates without hitting the non-letter condition to trigger the copy. Additionally, it does not null-terminate the strings in c properly if they are not initialized to zero, leading to garbage characters.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The logic for extracting words is flawed. It only processes a word when a non-letter character is encountered. If a line ends with a letter (which is common), the last word of that line is never extracted because the loop terminates without hitting the 'else' condition to trigger the copy. Additionally, it does not null-terminate the strings in c[s], leading to garbage data during comparison and output.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.2 , reason:  [This condition skips empty words correctly, but the loop does not handle the last word in a line if the line doesn't end with a non-letter character.]
